mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning among institutional investors, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment acceleration signal, or the signal shows no predictive power for weekly equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based economic expansion, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over 60-day windows, or the interaction term shows a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression of forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins for manufacturing-heavy S&P 500 constituents, yielding negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines coincide with positive S&P 500 forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the signal loses predictive power when adjusted for sector weightings.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the earnings base for public corporations, which mechanically lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP acceleration consistently correlates with negative or flat 5-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, invalidating the earnings-growth transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding a 3.0 percent annualized threshold erodes real purchasing power and forces aggressive central bank tightening, which depresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally persistently despite inflation readings above the 3.0 percent threshold, or forward returns show no statistical difference between high-inflation and low-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth deceleration and unemployment rate elevation confirm a labor market inflection point that precedes corporate earnings revisions downward, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor deterioration signal fails to predict equity drawdowns, or forward returns remain positive when both series move in the specified adverse direction.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in residential investment and housing activity signals a demand shock that rapidly transmits to corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week during credit cycle peaks.
failure_condition: Housing demand contractions do not lead to negative equity returns, or the signal produces false breakdowns during periods of accommodative financial conditions.
